channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-07-01
item.description:
Our open-source research community has seen exciting progress across multiple projects. The `giladfeldman/docpluck` project has released a new version, `v2.4.99`, with notable features such as region-driven table capture and Camelot capture, showcasing the team's dedication to improving document extraction capabilities. Meanwhile, the `shaheedazaad/auto-zcurve` project has enhanced its validation note with a new citation, demonstrating a commitment to academic rigor. Additionally, the `giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward` project has made its extension more accessible by adding a Chrome Webstore link, highlighting the connections between our projects and the community's focus on user experience. These updates demonstrate the collaborative spirit and ongoing innovation within our community, and we look forward to seeing the impact of these advancements. Overall, it's an exciting time for our community, with new features and improvements being released regularly.

41 commits across 4 active repositories this week.

Most active:

regcheck22 commits
metacheck7 commits
docpluck7 commits
escicheck5 commits

item.title: metacheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

The `ref_update` branch was worked on, where the `ref_accuracy` was updated and validated. The commit also included minor fixes to `reg_check` and `ref_consistency`, as stated in the commit message. No other branches were mentioned in the provided commits. The specific changes made were to `ref_accuracy`, `reg_check`, and `ref_consistency`, with the goal of validation and minor fixes. The work was done by Lakens, as indicated by the commit message. The commit occurred on 2026-06-30, marking the point at which these changes were made.

1 commit(s) today:

  • bb7a5ee [ref_update] updated ref_accuracy, validated. Minor fixes to reg_check and ref_consistency — Lakens
  • 6937e9c [dev] Merge exact_p_zero_warning: flag p-values reported as exactly zero — Lakens
  • 3c854b3 [dev] Rewrite OSF prereg extraction to schema-driven dispatch — Lakens
  • 32659a9 [main] Point home page text to the Metacheck Manual — Lakens
  • d6a7f12 [main] Replace Get Started/Reference/Articles navbar with Metacheck Manual link — Lakens
  • b8ac461 [dev] Fix coi_check/funding_check cold-start failure — Lakens
  • de41849 [dev] Add R/import-papers.R so dev installs cleanly — Lakens
  • ed8c8d3 [dev] Merge pull request #317 from scienceverse/papers_databases — Lisa DeBruine
  • e077134 [dev] Merge pull request #316 from scienceverse/ethics_check — Lisa DeBruine
  • a40d7db [dev] Merge pull request #309 from scienceverse/prereg_regcheck — Lisa DeBruine
  • 1fb2bab [dev] Merge pull request #318 from scienceverse/fix/code-check-paperlist — Lisa DeBruine
  • 8cbef44 [dev] Fix code_check crash and missing paper_id on paper lists — Lakens
  • b7afa6a [dev] add functions to load paper databases from scienceverse/papers — Lakens
  • 25c15a6 [main] update metacheck page to fix #314 — Lakens
  • 73736b0 [dev] Fix create_report_app() to report_app() in Get Started vignette — Daniel Lakens
  • 00d7953 [dev] add ethics_check + bug fix — Lakens
  • 2b8a367 [new_modules] Revert "fix app instruction #314" — Lakens
  • 1d3719b [new_modules] fix app instruction #314 — Lakens
  • dccb9f4 [main] update module names in vignettes — Lakens
  • 684ccdf [main] Update DESCRIPTION fix #313 — Daniel Lakens

View all commits →

item.title: regcheck: 9 commits today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

Work was done on the "staging" branch, where the sign-up feature was removed as per the commit message "remove sign-up". The user survey was also modified with an "improvement" as stated by Jamie Cummins. Aesthetic changes were made in two separate commits, with "minor aesthetic improvements" and "additional aesthetic improvements" being added. The functionality of the repository was also updated with the addition of "multi-file upload", "advanced error handling and regeneration", and an "html export for report" along with "various QOL improvements". Additionally, two fixes were made, including a fix for "quote tracing" and an enhancement to "report retention".

9 commit(s) today:

  • 1f6986b [staging] remove sign-up — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →

item.title: escicheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

Work was done on the 'main' branch, where a sync was performed from escicheckapp@928a5bc, specifically effectcheck version 0.6.11, as stated in the commit message "sync from escicheckapp@928a5bc (effectcheck v0.6.11)". Another commit on the 'main' branch involved syncing from escicheckapp@1d4c2dd, which corresponds to effectcheck version 0.6.9, according to the commit message "sync from escicheckapp@1d4c2dd (effectcheck v0.6.9)". These commits, issued on consecutive days, updated the 'main' branch with the latest changes from the escicheckapp repository, incorporating effectcheck versions 0.6.9 and 0.6.11. The changes brought into the 'main' branch are tied to specific versions of effectcheck, indicating a dependency on this external project. No other branches were modified in this set of commits.

1 commit(s) today:

  • bedc262 [main] sync from escicheckapp@928a5bc (effectcheck v0.6.11) (#24) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →
